findings.title: lastOrderAt uses createdAt.max() across ALL order statuses, not PAID/SERVED — diverges from 14d ended-season spec
findings.why_it_matters: Plan §19 explicitly defines ended season as '마지막 PAID/SERVED 주문 후 14일 무활동'. Implementation computes lastOrderAt as max(order.createdAt) over the entire orders set with no status filter and no transition-time tracking. Two concrete deviations: (1) a workspace whose only recent activity is CANCELLED or stuck-NOT_PAID orders is treated as still-active and excluded from the distribution, even though no real revenue happened; (2) createdAt is order creation time, not the PAID transition time — an order created months ago that just transitioned to PAID is invisible to this heuristic, and conversely an order created yesterday that was immediately cancelled keeps the workspace 'active'. Both effects bias the distribution: the population of 'ended' workspaces gets smaller and skewed (away from workspaces with cancellation churn), inflating percentiles for active workspaces being ranked.
findings.evidence: SeasonInsightRepository.kt:45 and :89 — `val lastOrderAt = order.createdAt.max()` with no status predicate and no separate `paidAt`/transition column reference. Compare to plan line 19 ('마지막 PAID/SERVED 주문 후 14일 무활동') and SeasonDistributionService.kt:25 cutoff logic that consumes this value as if it were a paid-transition timestamp.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the max() with a CASE/conditional so only PAID/SERVED rows participate, e.g. `Expressions.dateTimeTemplate(LocalDateTime::class.java, "max(case when {0} in ({1}, {2}) then {3} else null end)", order.status, OrderStatus.PAID, OrderStatus.SERVED, order.createdAt)`. Even better, expose and use a paid-transition timestamp if one exists. Confirm the chosen semantics with the spec author before merging.

findings.title: businessDayCount counts dates of CANCELLED-only orders, contradicting '취소 제외' aggregation pattern used for sales
findings.why_it_matters: businessDayCount uses `count(distinct cast(order.createdAt as date))` over the unfiltered order set, while totalSales/totalOrders/cancelledOrders are all conditional on status. A workspace with a single CANCELLED order on a given day will record businessDayCount=1, totalOrders=0, totalSales=0. Downstream, SeasonMetricsCalculator.turnoverRate divides totalOrders/businessDayCount, which is fine when totalOrders=0 (numerator zero), but the asymmetry inflates the denominator in mixed cases (some real days + some cancel-only days), pushing turnoverRate downward and hurting the workspace's percentile ranking on TURNOVER_RATE in StrengthSelector.
findings.evidence: SeasonInsightRepository.kt:40-44 (and :84-88) — businessDayCount has no `case when notCancelled` wrapper, unlike totalSales/totalOrders defined on lines 25-39. SeasonMetricsCalculator.kt:13 — `a.totalOrders.toDouble() / a.businessDayCount / a.tableCount`.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap with the same predicate: `count(distinct case when {0} then cast({1} as date) else null end)` with `notCancelled` as the predicate, so only revenue-bearing days count. Apply consistently in both aggregateForWorkspace and aggregateAll.

findings.title: MilestoneTier distribution includes workspaces below the 1M floor used to gate the requesting workspace, inflating percentiles
findings.why_it_matters: resolve() short-circuits when the requester's cumulativeSales < 1,000,000 KRW (the policy floor), but the distribution it ranks against (`distribution.totalSales`) is built from ALL ended workspaces by SeasonDistributionService — including workspaces whose own season totals were below 1M. A real 1.5M workspace ranked against a population dominated by sub-1M test/abandoned workspaces will be falsely promoted to TOP_30/TOP_10. If the spec intent is 'rank only against meaningful seasons', the floor should be applied symmetrically when building the distribution.
findings.evidence: MilestoneTierResolver.kt:16-19 — guard `cumulativeSales < MILESTONE_FLOOR_KRW` only protects requester. SeasonDistributionService.kt:46 — `totalSales = ended.map { it.totalSales }.sorted()` with no floor. Test fixture in MilestoneTierResolverTest.kt:14 includes 100K..10M values, demonstrating the unfiltered population.
findings.suggested_fix: If symmetric semantics are intended, filter `ended` by `it.totalSales >= MilestoneTierResolver.MILESTONE_FLOOR_KRW` before building distribution.totalSales (and probably the metric distributions too, since sub-1M workspaces are noise). Confirm intent with product owner — current behavior may be deliberate for the 'experimental BM' framing.

findings.title: Shared mockk instances across Given blocks with eager service calls leak stub state if any future test reuses workspaceId
findings.why_it_matters: BehaviorSpec evaluates Given block bodies eagerly during spec construction. The single `insightRepo` and `distService` mockk instances are shared across all four Givens, and `service.getInsight(workspaceId)` is invoked outside any Then block (lines 43, 93, 122) so it executes during construction and captures the response then. Currently safe because each Given uses a distinct workspaceId (7,9,10,11) and `distService.computeDistribution()` stubs are overwritten in declaration order before each eager call. However this is fragile: any added Given that reuses 7L/9L/10L/11L, or any reordering, will silently mix stubs. The same fragile pattern exists in SeasonDistributionServiceTest.kt and is the class of bug the persona instructions flagged for this branch.
findings.evidence: SeasonInsightServiceTest.kt:12-13 (shared mocks declared once at spec scope), :43, :93, :122 (eager service calls inside Given but outside Then). SeasonDistributionServiceTest.kt:14, :40, :61, :91 same pattern.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) move `every {}` stubs and `service.getInsight(...)` invocations inside `When { ... }` / `Then { ... }` blocks so they execute per-test, or (b) call `clearMocks(insightRepo, distService)` at the start of each Given and re-stub. The existing SeasonInsightFacadeTest already uses `clearMocks` on line 41 — apply the same discipline here.

findings.title: SeasonInsightServiceTest 'topAvgOrderAmount' Then assertion may be evaluated against stale eager-captured response when spec is re-ordered
findings.why_it_matters: Same root cause as the shared-mock finding, but with a concrete blast radius: response on line 122 is captured during construction with stub from line 113. If a future Given is inserted between this Given's stub on line 113 and its eager call on line 122 (or if Kotest changes ordering), the response will reflect a different distribution and the topSales=10_310_000 / topAvgOrder=30_000 / topTurnover=152.7 assertions on lines 124-131 will silently pass against wrong data or fail mysteriously.
findings.evidence: SeasonInsightServiceTest.kt:113 (`every { distService.computeDistribution() } returns ...`), :122 (`val response = service.getInsight(workspaceId)` outside Then), :124-131 assertions on captured response.
findings.suggested_fix: Move both the `every` stub and the `service.getInsight` call inside a When or the first Then block so they execute together at test time. Repeat for the other three Givens in this file.

residual_risks: ClockConfiguration hardcodes Asia/Seoul — fine for current single-region deployment, but if/when JPA entities are migrated to Instant/OffsetDateTime, the LocalDateTime-vs-zoned-clock mixing will silently shift the 14d cutoff. Out of scope for this diff.

@Cacheable on SeasonInsightService (5m) and SeasonDistributionService (1h) have no eviction wired to order/workspace mutations. Donations/social-proof can lag up to 5min and distribution up to 1h. Acceptable per polling-migration intent but worth confirming with PM that 1h staleness on social-proof headlines is OK.

Distribution metric lists (avgOrderAmount, turnoverRate, tableRevenue, lowCancellationRate) can be empty even when populationSize > 0, because each list filters mapNotNull on its respective metric. PercentileCalculator returns 100 for empty distributions, which correctly excludes the metric from strengths but means StrengthSelector silently drops candidates without surfacing a 'distribution insufficient' signal to clients.
testing_gaps: No test for SeasonInsightRepository.aggregateForWorkspace / aggregateAll — the QueryDSL CASE/COALESCE templates and the `count(distinct cast(... as date))` SQL function are unverified against an actual DB. The two correctness findings above (lastOrderAt status filter, businessDayCount status filter) would have been caught by an integration test.

No test for the 100만원 floor interaction with a tier-eligible distribution — i.e., a workspace at exactly 1,000,000 KRW where the distribution puts it in TOP_50. The existing tests check 999,999 (below floor) and 5,000,000 / 9,300,000 (well above) but skip the boundary.

ClockConfiguration has no test verifying LocalDateTime.now(clock) returns Asia/Seoul wall time and that the SeasonDistributionService 14-day cutoff respects it during DST or year-boundary edge cases (KST has no DST, but documenting this assumption is worth a test).
